notes_md:
**Người nhà** means *family member* — someone in your family. It can refer to one person or your relatives in general, depending on context.  

It’s a noun; you can say *một người nhà* (one family member), but often it’s used collectively without a number. In places like hospitals, **người nhà** often means the patient’s relatives (not the patient).  

Close to **thành viên gia đình**, but **người nhà** feels more personal and relationship-focused. Different from **gia đình**, which means the whole family as a unit.
notes:
Người nhà means family member — someone in your family. It can refer to one person or your relatives in general, depending on context.  

It’s a noun; you can say một người nhà (one family member), but often it’s used collectively without a number. In places like hospitals, người nhà often means the patient’s relatives (not the patient).  

Close to thành viên gia đình, but người nhà feels more personal and relationship-focused. Different from gia đình, which means the whole family as a unit.
exampleSentences: Hôm qua anh đưa người nhà đi khám bệnh ở bệnh viện.
Chị thường gọi điện về nhà để hỏi thăm người nhà mỗi tuần.
Nếu em cần giúp đỡ, người nhà luôn sẵn sàng hỗ trợ em.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Yesterday I took a family member to the hospital for a check-up.
She often calls home to check on a family member every week.
If you need help, a family member is always ready to support you.
